hero.title: Urban Strife Wiki
hero.description: Lead a militia through a zombie-ravaged Southern town, explore dangerous locations, rebuild Urban Shelter, craft weapons, recruit survivors, and choose between rival factions.

gameFeature.title: Urban Strife Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Lead turn-based tactical battles with simulated ballistics, rebuild Urban Shelter, train survivors, and pick a side between rival factions before the Atlanta horde arrives.

urbanStrifeReleaseInfo.title: Urban Strife Release Date, Price and PC Requirements
urbanStrifeReleaseInfo.subtitle: Urban Strife Version 1.0 launched on Steam for Windows PC on July 14, 2026. The complete release adds the campaign conclusion, the final Shelter siege and several major progression systems.
urbanStrifeReleaseInfo.intro: Urban Strife entered Steam Early Access on December 10, 2024 before reaching Version 1.0 on July 14, 2026. The full release completes the Atlanta Horde storyline, adds the final Shelter defense sequence, introduces equipment repairs and redesigns character profession perks.

specs.details: The complete game launched after roughly nineteen months in Steam Early Access.
Early Access introduced the core campaign, tactical combat, faction quests, exploration and Shelter management while later story and endgame content was still in development.
Urban Strife is a single-player game with Steam Cloud, Steam Workshop and Family Sharing support. No console or macOS edition is listed.
Steam applies regional pricing in supported countries, so the final local price may differ.
The introductory Steam offer runs through July 21, 2026.

specs.value: English, French, German, Spanish, Russian, Simplified Chinese
specs.details: English includes interface text, subtitles and full audio. French, German, Spanish, Russian and Simplified Chinese include translated interfaces and subtitles.
Intel Core i5-4690 or AMD FX-8350, Nvidia GTX 970 or AMD RX 480 with at least 4 GB VRAM, and DirectX 11.
Intel Core i7-8700K or AMD Ryzen 5 3600, Nvidia RTX 2060 Super or AMD RX 5700 XT with at least 8 GB VRAM, DirectX 11 and 20 GB storage.

specs.value: Atlanta Horde and final Shelter siege
specs.details: Version 1.0 concludes the main story with the horde attacking Urban Shelter. Fortifications, powered upgrades, stored supplies and recruited allies all contribute to the Shelter Defense Tracker.
Weapons and body armor can be repaired after starting the repair operation. Profession perks are divided into three tiers and can be freely selected after unlocking them.
Early Access saves were not wiped, although beginning a fresh campaign is recommended for the most complete Version 1.0 experience.

urbanStrifeBeginnerGuide.title: Urban Strife Beginner Guide and First-Hours Route
urbanStrifeBeginnerGuide.intro: The early campaign introduces equipment slots, tactical movement, zombie spawners, scavenging and Shelter management. Progress carefully through the Prologue, return valuable supplies to the Shelter and avoid treating every encounter as a full gunfight.

steps.title: Begin with a Fresh Version 1.0 Campaign
steps.description: Create your character and follow the opening story in which the CDC patient is rescued and brought to Urban Shelter. A new campaign uses the current quest flow, perk system and blueprint distribution.
tips: Read the tutorial windows instead of closing them immediately; completed help screens can be reopened from the HUD.
Use the opening sequence to learn quick slots, equipment management and basic interaction controls.

steps.title: Complete the Bridge and Prologue Tutorials
steps.description: The introductory route teaches equipping items, movement, combat actions and the destruction of zombie spawners. Finish these objectives before freely exploring harder locations.
tips: Equip healing items and weapons in accessible slots before combat begins.
Destroy active spawners when the mission permits it so they cannot keep adding zombies to the battle.

steps.title: Inspect Urban Shelter Before Leaving
steps.description: Urban Shelter is the central base for storage, production, recovery and long-term defense. Review the Shelter Notebook, available upgrades and resource categories before selecting your first away team.
tips: Food, medicine, morale supplies, fuel, power and construction materials all support different Shelter systems.
The radio, hospital, auto shop, workshop and barracks are important parts of the wider rebuilding plan.

steps.title: Prepare a Balanced Away Team
steps.description: Use the roster screen to confirm who is leaving the Shelter. A practical early group combines accurate ranged attacks, quiet melee options and access to medical treatment.
tips: Give each fighter a usable weapon instead of placing every valuable gun on one character.
Carry enough medicine to stop bleeding and treat serious injuries, but leave surplus supplies in the Shelter stash.
Check ammunition types before departure because carrying a gun without compatible rounds wastes inventory space.

steps.title: Clear Nearby Prologue Areas First
steps.description: Explore the Shelter surroundings and early suburban sectors before challenging stronger faction locations. The world-map sector panel identifies previously visited areas and displays the main local faction difficulty.
tips: Finish nearby objectives and return with supplies rather than pushing through several unknown sectors in one trip.
Search containers, abandoned buildings and defeated enemies after an area is secure.
Avoid carrying so much loot that the team cannot keep essential ammunition or medical items.

steps.title: Fight Isolated Zombies Quietly
steps.description: Use melee weapons, crossbows or suppressed firearms when a loud attack could alert additional enemies. Gunfire, explosions and careless movement can pull more threats into the encounter.
tips: Approach while crouched and use cover to reduce the risk of losing the initiative.
Use high-percentage attacks instead of repeatedly attempting difficult headshots.
Save shotguns, automatic fire and explosives for groups that justify their ammunition cost.

steps.title: Protect Medicine, Ammunition and Durability
steps.description: Urban Strife tracks ammunition, injuries and equipment condition. Use the cheapest safe solution for each encounter and avoid changing attachments during combat unless the tactical benefit is worth the Action Point cost.
tips: Do not fire expensive rifle or shotgun ammunition at a target that can be safely finished with melee.
Stop bleeding quickly because untreated wounds keep reducing health.
Keep backup weapons instead of relying on one damaged firearm for the entire expedition.

steps.title: Salvage Surplus Equipment Carefully
steps.description: Unwanted weapons and armor can be salvaged into parts used for repairs and Shelter development. Version 1.0 excludes equipped items from salvage by default, helping protect the gear currently used by the party.
tips: Preserve useful spare guns until you know which ammunition and weapon classes your recruits need.
Salvaging equipment before it becomes completely ruined can produce more useful material.
Keep crafting tools available because using them can consume durability.

steps.title: Return, Recover and Upgrade
steps.description: Deposit loot in the Shelter stash, treat injured survivors and invest materials in useful facilities. Once the repair operation is available, damaged weapons and body armor can be restored with salvaged parts.
tips: Repair equipment you regularly use instead of spending every part on low-value backup items.
Ballistic plates are expendable and cannot be repaired, even though the body armor holding them can be restored.
Maintain powered defenses and stockpiles as the campaign moves toward the Atlanta Horde siege.

urbanStrifeCombatControls.title: Urban Strife Combat, Overwatch and Controls Guide
urbanStrifeCombatControls.intro: Urban Strife uses turn-based Action Point combat backed by simulated bullet trajectories, material penetration and individual explosion fragments. Positioning, noise and available reactions are as important as raw weapon damage.

steps.title: Plan the Entire Turn Around Action Points
steps.description: Movement, attacks and combat interactions consume Action Points. Preview a destination before moving so the selected character still has enough points to attack, take cover or prepare Overwatch.
steps.example: Move only as far as needed to gain a clear firing angle instead of spending the full turn crossing open ground.

steps.title: Win Initiative from Cover
steps.description: Initiative determines which side reacts first when contact begins. Crouched characters positioned in cover are less likely to suffer an enemy ambush, while standing or running into detection is much riskier.
steps.example: Approach a guarded building while crouched behind vehicles or walls, then begin the fight from a protected firing position.

steps.title: Use Cover Based on Its Material
steps.description: Cover effectiveness depends on material, thickness and firing angle. A car engine can stop most common calibers, while a thin wall may be penetrated by a powerful hunting rifle.
steps.example: Hide behind the engine block rather than a car door, and do not assume wooden walls provide complete protection from rifles.

steps.title: Check Hit Chance and Expected Damage
steps.description: The targeting interface estimates hit chance and potential damage while accounting for range, obstruction and bullet attenuation. Reposition when the preview shows poor odds instead of wasting ammunition.
steps.example: A short flank that removes a wall or cover penalty can be more valuable than firing twice from a bad angle.

steps.title: Choose the Correct Body Part
steps.description: Headshots offer high payoff with lower accuracy, center-mass attacks provide a safer hit chance, and limb attacks can cripple, slow or disable a target.
steps.example: Use a torso shot when a wounded enemy must be stopped immediately, or target a limb when disabling the target matters more than maximum damage.

steps.title: Control Noise and Detection
steps.description: Enemy awareness is influenced by dynamic sight and hearing ranges. Melee weapons, crossbows and suppressed pistols support quiet eliminations, while unsuppressed gunfire and explosives can attract additional enemies.
steps.example: Silently remove an isolated guard before using loud weapons against the main group.

steps.title: Prepare Interrupts and Overwatch
steps.description: Overwatch creates a reaction opportunity during the enemy turn. Place the character where enemies must cross a clear firing lane and avoid blocking that lane with teammates.
steps.example: Aim Overwatch toward a doorway or narrow street, then allow advancing enemies to enter the prepared line of fire.

steps.title: Fight Human Enemies with Flanks and Penetration
steps.description: Human opponents use cover, reposition and read the battlefield. Break their firing angles, attack from more than one direction and use suitable calibers or explosives against weak cover.
steps.example: Keep one fighter covering the front while a second character enters through a window or moves around the side of the building.

steps.title: Handle Individual Zombies Efficiently
steps.description: Avoid spending high-value ammunition on a lone zombie when a controlled melee attack, crossbow bolt or accurate low-cost shot can end the encounter without attracting a larger group.
steps.example: Use a quiet weapon on the first zombie and keep a firearm ready in case the attack reveals additional enemies.

steps.title: Defend Against Large Hordes
steps.description: Horde zombies move together during a dedicated simultaneous phase while making individual pathing decisions. Use choke points, overlapping firing lanes, shotguns, automatic fire and area explosives before the group surrounds the party.
steps.example: Retreat through a narrow entrance, place ranged fighters behind cover and concentrate damage on the front of the horde.

shortcuts.action: Swap the selected character's active weapon
Change the current weapon's available fire mode
Select the entire party for group movement outside combat

urbanStrifeBestWeapons.title: Urban Strife Best Weapons and Gear by Tactical Role
urbanStrifeBestWeapons.intro: There is no single weapon that solves every encounter. Pistols conserve Action Points, rifles reward range and penetration, shotguns dominate close groups, silent equipment limits enemy alerts, and protective gear trades durability for survival.

tiers.weapons: Shotguns, machine guns, automatic and burst-fire weapons, grenades and Molotov cocktails
tiers.bestFor: Dense zombie groups, chokepoints and Shelter defense
tiers.strengths: Shotguns calculate every pellet separately and can deliver heavy close-range damage. Burst fire provides cumulative damage and can penetrate multiple targets, while explosives create fragments or area denial.
tiers.limitations: These options are loud, consume ammunition quickly and can attract enemies from a wider area.

tiers.weapons: Hunting rifles, sniper rifles and the rare .50 BMG anti-materiel rifle
tiers.bestFor: Armored targets, human marksmen and enemies behind penetrable cover
tiers.strengths: High-powered rifles deliver strong single-target damage and can penetrate materials that stop weaker weapons. The rare .50 BMG rifle can destroy cover and fire through substantial obstacles.
tiers.limitations: Rifles are less convenient at close range and should not waste valuable ammunition on weak isolated enemies.

tiers.bestFor: Stealth routes, isolated guards and quiet zombie removal
tiers.strengths: These weapons allow ranged attacks without producing the noise of standard firearms. Crossbows support silent play without conventional ammunition, while pistols remain quick to use.
tiers.limitations: They offer less crowd control than shotguns, automatic weapons or explosives and can struggle when several enemies attack at once.

tiers.bestFor: Finishing wounded targets, mobile fighters and emergency backup
tiers.strengths: Pistols are the game's low-Action-Point firearm class, making them useful when a character must move and still attack during the same turn.
tiers.limitations: Sidearms generally provide less damage, range and cover penetration than rifles or dedicated heavy weapons.

tiers.weapons: Blades, blunt weapons, baseball bats, improvised tools and unarmed attacks
tiers.bestFor: Silent takedowns, ammunition conservation and close zombie encounters
tiers.strengths: Melee attacks create less noise and require no ammunition. Blunt attacks can damage stamina and support non-lethal knockouts against suitable targets.
tiers.limitations: The attacker must enter dangerous close range and may be exposed if the first strike fails.

tiers.weapons: Fragmentation grenades, homemade explosives, Molotov cocktails and specialty bombs
tiers.bestFor: Enemies behind light cover, grouped targets and blocked approaches
tiers.strengths: Explosions generate individual fragments, can destroy or bypass cover and force enemies away from protected positions. Molotovs help control routes used by advancing groups.
tiers.limitations: Explosives are limited resources, create substantial noise and can endanger nearby party members.

tiers.weapons: Body armor, ballistic plates, limb armor and helmets
tiers.bestFor: Frontline fighters, exposed movement and prolonged firefights
tiers.strengths: Armor absorbs incoming damage and reduces the risk faced by characters holding close positions or crossing contested areas. Body armor can be repaired through the Version 1.0 repair system.
tiers.limitations: Armor plates and limb protection decay when hit. Ballistic plates are expendable and cannot be repaired.

tiers.weapons: Silencers, weapon attachments, tactical weapon kits and high-capacity magazines
tiers.bestFor: Specializing a reliable weapon for stealth, accuracy or sustained fire
tiers.strengths: Attachments, kits and magazines improve the tactical role of equipment and can be looted, fabricated or produced through Shelter facilities.
tiers.limitations: Attaching or detaching equipment during combat consumes Action Points, so changes should normally be completed before contact.

urbanStrifeCharacterBuilds.title: Urban Strife Character Builds, Skills and Perks
urbanStrifeCharacterBuilds.intro: There is no mandatory class setup. Decide what each survivor should do most often, give that character matching weapons and field responsibilities, and let repeated actions reinforce the attributes needed for that role before selecting supporting profession perks.

roles.bestFor: Reliable firearm damage, Overwatch lanes and priority-target removal
roles.focus: Make ranged attacks regularly instead of carrying a firearm only as a backup. Marksmanship sets ranged aim accuracy while Agility affects movement Action Points, interrupt chances and melee evasion.
roles.plan: Use cover, elevation and clean firing lanes. Reserve expensive shots for important targets and keep enough ammunition for the late campaign.

roles.bestFor: Silent kills, ammunition conservation and protecting fragile squad members
roles.focus: Use the melee family that matches the intended job: blade or pierce, blunt, hand-to-hand or heavy melee. Strength raises carry capacity, melee damage and stamina, Dexterity affects item-use Action Points and melee accuracy, and Endurance increases health and stamina.
roles.plan: Approach through cover or stealth, avoid open firing lanes and carry a low-Action-Point backup weapon for emergencies.

roles.bestFor: Scouting, silent takedowns, opening attacks and avoiding unnecessary alerts
roles.focus: Sneak frequently, since sneaking and successful melee dodges contribute to Agility growth. Combine silent melee weapons, crossbows or suppressed firearms with ambushes from cover before combat fully escalates.
roles.plan: Scout first, isolate a target, secure an escape route and let the rest of the squad cover the withdrawal.

roles.bestFor: Healing, emergency rescues and keeping injured fighters operational
roles.focus: Make this survivor the regular user of medical supplies instead of spreading healing actions randomly across the squad. Dexterity reduces item-use Action Points, Intelligence speeds all attribute growth and enables Intelligence dialogue checks, and Endurance gives more health and stamina for dangerous rescues.
roles.plan: End turns behind solid cover, keep movement points available and avoid spending the medic's entire turn on low-value attacks.

roles.bestFor: Carrying loot, crafting, grenade use, specialist tools and alternate quest solutions
roles.focus: Use the survivor for encumbered carrying, backpack crafting, grenades and other repeatable utility actions, and bring the character into conversations where faction background, Intelligence or Strength can reveal alternative information and quest solutions.
roles.plan: Keep tools and mission items organized on this character, but distribute critical ammunition and medicine so one knockout does not disable the whole squad.

urbanStrifeShelterUpgrades.title: Urban Strife Shelter Upgrades and Crafting
urbanStrifeShelterUpgrades.intro: Upgrade in layers: stabilize daily needs first, restore facilities that unlock essential services, expand production and crafting, and then commit resources to faction bonuses and siege defenses. Crafting can draw directly from the base stash and loot containers, while hidden recipes come from exploration, unusual NPCs and faction alliances.

phases.focus: Food, water security, health supplies, morale and power
facilities: Donation flow for food, health and morale items
phases.goal: Return from every safe trip with food, medicine, morale items, fuel and general materials instead of carrying only weapons.

phases.focus: Repair damaged town-center facilities that open core campaign functions
phases.goal: Prioritize construction materials and specialist access until communication, medical care, vehicle work, crafting and team facilities are operational.

phases.focus: Reduce dependence on scavenging by producing necessities at home
phases.goal: Spend early surplus on repeatable production before decorative projects or narrow specialty upgrades.

phases.phase: 4. Build the Arsenal and Crafting Chain
phases.focus: Turn common loot and materials into combat-ready equipment
phases.goal: Keep chemicals, components, metal, spare weapons and useful attachments instead of selling or discarding every duplicate.

phases.phase: 5. Add Training, Mobility and Scavenging Capacity
phases.focus: Improve the quality and reach of field operations
phases.goal: Balance fuel consumption against the need to reach harder maps, haul more loot and rescue threatened settlements.

phases.focus: Convert political progress into permanent Shelter advantages
phases.goal: Secure an alliance, collect faction rewards and maintain enough power capacity for every defense that must remain active.

phases.focus: Stop expanding and convert remaining resources into survival
phases.goal: Repair the best weapons and armor, salvage unwanted gear for repair parts, stock ammunition and place the strongest fighters on the final roster.

urbanStrifeFactions.title: Urban Strife Factions, Reputation and Recruits
urbanStrifeFactions.intro: The three major blocs are the National Guard, the Church of Second Chance and the Shady Lady biker charter. Smaller settlements, barons and recruitable survivors also react to your actions, and their survival can affect votes, map control, rewards and the strength of roaming undead hordes.

factions.alias: Earlier known as the Rogue Army Garrison
factions.identity: A disciplined military power based in a heavily fortified mountain base that claims to protect Urban while demanding obedience, payment and loyalty.
perks: Faction vehicle access including the Army HMMWV
factions.alliance: An accepted alliance opens faction upgrades, rewards and late-game support, but places the Shelter against rival blocs.

factions.alias: Also called the Cult of Second Chance
factions.identity: Father Walker's movement at the former juvenile re-education facility treats the post-pandemic world as a spiritual rebirth and embraces the undead in ways other survivors find dangerous.
perks: Access to the faction's hidden camp and leadership
factions.alliance: The Church can become the Shelter's chosen ally, opening its story route and benefits while making its enemies part of the final political cost.

factions.identity: A profit-driven faction built around the Shady Lady biker bar, focused on deals, entertainment, vehicle work, weapons and black-market trade.
perks: Biker traders, faction gear and vehicle-related rewards
factions.alliance: A biker alliance adds its own upgrade and equipment path to the Shelter and determines which rival factions will oppose you.

factions.alias: Firefighters, refugees, federal groups, independent camps and regional strongmen
factions.identity: Smaller groups scattered across settlements, public buildings and fortified sector maps have their own needs, routines and moral limits. They may become voters, refugees, traders, recruits, enemies or victims of roaming hordes.
perks: Sector access, supplies and local information
factions.alliance: Their votes and survival help determine access to major faction headquarters and how much of Urban remains alive when the endgame begins.

factions.identity: Personal reputation follows the player into later conversations, while recruit backgrounds can make strangers more willing to reveal information.
perks: Different story paths and Shelter upgrades
Vehicles, advanced blueprints and more than twenty ally upgrades
factions.alliance: After securing the required votes, the chosen faction opens its headquarters. Accepting the alliance commits the Shelter to that ally for the final conflict.

urbanStrifeStoryWalkthrough.title: Urban Strife Story Walkthrough and Horde Endgame
urbanStrifeStoryWalkthrough.intro: Progress is not a single fixed quest line. Exploration order, rescued groups, faction reputation, Shelter upgrades and alliance decisions all feed into the final act, where the Defense Tracker measures the strength of your fortifications, supplies, allies and powered upgrades.

stages.heading: Prologue: The Patient from Atlanta
objectives: Survive the opening after being evacuated by the CDC from an Atlanta laboratory.
Recover at Urban Shelter and learn the basic tactical, inventory and survival systems.
Meet the aging residents who rescued the player and understand why the Shelter needs outside help.
decisions: Define the player character's early combat and dialogue strengths.
Preserve scarce medicine and ammunition while collecting food, morale and construction supplies.
stages.readiness: Leave the opening area with working weapons, healing supplies and enough carrying capacity to return useful Shelter resources.

stages.heading: Early Campaign: Restore the Shelter
objectives: Repair the city radio, hospital, auto shop, workshop and barracks.
Recruit local survivors and form a balanced field team.
Stabilize food, health, morale, fuel and construction-material supplies.
decisions: Choose which damaged service receives materials first.
Decide whether to invest in immediate combat equipment or long-term production such as gardens and specialist facilities.
stages.readiness: Core services should be operating and the squad should complete nearby Tier 1 encounters without consuming the entire reserve.

stages.heading: County Campaign: Survivor Conflicts and Faction Votes
objectives: Explore increasingly dangerous sectors farther from the Shelter.
Meet independent survivor groups, faction barons, federal remnants, cultists, bikers and soldiers.
Use combat, bargaining, faction affinity, Strength checks or Intelligence checks to resolve local conflicts.
decisions: Choose which groups to save, recruit, expose, defeat or leave behind.
Protect useful settlements when roaming undead begin attacking human-controlled sectors.
Build enough support to earn the votes required for access to a major faction headquarters.
stages.readiness: Maintain fuel, cargo space and spare equipment for distant maps while keeping the Shelter supplied.

stages.heading: Alliance Phase: Choose a Major Faction
objectives: Enter the headquarters of the National Guard, Church of Second Chance or Shady Lady bikers after securing the required votes.
Meet the faction leader, uncover the group's deeper motives and decide whether to accept the alliance.
Trade discovered secrets and complete alliance content for powerful rewards.
decisions: Select which faction will stand with the Shelter when the Atlanta horde arrives.
Accept the political and military consequences of opposing the other major blocs.
stages.readiness: Review the ally's upgrade branch, traders, blueprints and vehicle options before spending remaining late-game materials.

stages.heading: Final Story Episode: The Fall of the King
objectives: Travel to the fallen kingdom built around Urban High School.
Investigate the self-sustaining community and its plan to challenge the Republic.
Explore the school and football-field areas and resolve the last major regional story conflict.
decisions: Decide how the leader and his followers fit into the wider faction struggle.
Finish unresolved story business before committing all resources to the siege.
stages.readiness: Complete desired faction missions, recruitments and blueprint rewards before the campaign enters its final preparation window.

stages.heading: Horde Escalation: Urban Is Reclaimed by the Dead
objectives: Respond to moving hordes created in the wake of human conflicts.
Defend threatened camps or clear sectors that have already fallen.
Prevent compromised settlements from generating additional hordes that spread across the county.
decisions: Choose which calls for help are worth the travel time, ammunition and fuel.
Accept that arriving late can turn powerful human NPCs into armored undead carrying valuable equipment.
stages.readiness: Keep a mobile response team, spare fuel and anti-horde ammunition while finishing Shelter upgrades.

stages.heading: Final Preparation: Check the Defense Tracker
objectives: Review the Shelter's Defense Tracker before the assault.
Improve fortifications, supplies, allies and final upgrades.
Keep powered defenses online and place the strongest available fighters on the siege roster.
decisions: Stop low-value expansion and turn remaining resources into ammunition, medicine, food and repair parts.
Repair weapons and body armor, and salvage unwanted equipment for parts.
Decide which equipment must be preserved because ballistic plates cannot be repaired.
stages.readiness: The Version 1.0 campaign schedules the siege for Day 20 and sends a radio warning 24 in-game hours before it begins.

stages.heading: Atlanta Horde Siege and Ending Sequence
objectives: Defend the Shelter through the final siege missions.
Use prepared firing positions, repaired equipment, stored ammunition and faction support against the incoming mass of undead.
Protect the community with the facilities and defenses built throughout the campaign.
decisions: Commit limited fighters and supplies where they have the greatest effect.
Respond to final sacrifices or emergency choices created by weak defenses or missing resources.
stages.readiness: Normal scavenging can no longer solve shortages once the siege is underway. The Shelter survives on the people, stockpiles, power and upgrades already secured.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Urban Strife

questions.question: What is Urban Strife?
questions.answer: Urban Strife is a post-apocalyptic tactical survival RPG on Steam. It blends turn-based Action Point combat with simulated ballistics, Shelter management, survivor recruitment and faction alliances set in a zombie-ravaged Southern town.

questions.question: When did Urban Strife release?
questions.answer: Urban Strife reached Version 1.0 on July 14, 2026, after entering Steam Early Access on December 10, 2024. The full release completes the Atlanta Horde storyline, adds the final Shelter siege and introduces equipment repairs.

questions.question: Where can I play Urban Strife?
questions.answer: Urban Strife is available on Steam for Windows PC, with Steam Cloud, Steam Workshop and Family Sharing support. No console or macOS edition is currently listed.

questions.question: How much does Urban Strife cost?
questions.answer: Urban Strife is priced at $34.99 USD on Steam, with a 30% launch discount bringing it to $24.49 USD through July 21, 2026. Steam applies regional pricing in supported countries.

questions.question: Is Urban Strife a single-player game?
questions.answer: Yes. Urban Strife is a single-player experience with no multiplayer or co-op. You lead a militia, rebuild Urban Shelter and choose a faction alliance before the Atlanta horde arrives.

questions.question: What languages does Urban Strife support?
questions.answer: Urban Strife supports English with full audio, plus French, German, Spanish, Russian and Simplified Chinese with translated interfaces and subtitles, for six supported languages in total.

biomes.metaDescription: Explore Urban Strife maps including Urban County, Little Italy, Old Port, Adams Villa and hidden sectors with loot routes and danger notes.
biomes.whatIsDescription: Guides to Urban Strife map sectors, location layouts, resource distribution and danger zones. Learn what to expect and how to prepare for each area of Urban County.

whySteps.step1Description: Locate food, medicine, fuel and rare components across each sector
whySteps.step2Description: Understand faction territory, spawners and roaming horde paths
whySteps.step3Description: Prepare the right fuel, cargo space and equipment for distant maps

building.title: Urban Strife Shelter Building
building.description: Master Shelter facilities, production industries, crafting chains and siege defenses in Urban Strife.
building.metaDescription: Master Urban Strife Shelter building with facility upgrades, survival industries, crafting chains, powered defenses and siege readiness planning.
building.whatIsDescription: Guides to Urban Strife Shelter facilities, production industries, crafting chains, power systems and fortifications. Learn to build and defend your home base through the final siege.

whySteps.step1Description: Secure food, water, medicine, morale and power for daily operations
whySteps.step2Description: Produce necessities at home so scavenging is no longer the only supply line
whySteps.step3Description: Power fortifications and stockpile supplies before the Atlanta horde

support.title: Urban Strife Support
support.description: Technical support, PC requirements, performance optimization and troubleshooting for common Urban Strife issues.
support.metaTitle: Urban Strife Support - Technical Help & Fixes
support.metaDescription: Get technical support for Urban Strife including PC requirements, performance optimization, crash fixes and platform compatibility guidance.
support.whatIsDescription: Technical guides covering Urban Strife PC requirements, performance optimization, crash fixes and platform compatibility. Get help with settings, saves and common issues.

whySteps.step1Description: Resolve crashes, performance problems and compatibility issues
whySteps.step2Description: Configure settings for the best experience on your hardware
whySteps.step3Description: Learn about Steam Cloud, Workshop, Family Sharing and save support
